Compute Haplotype Diversity Metrics Per LD Block

Source: R/haplotypes.R
compute_haplotype_diversity.Rd

For each block produced by extract_haplotypes, computes:

Richness (\(k\))

Number of unique haplotype strings.

Expected heterozygosity (\(H_e\))

Nei's (1973) gene diversity: \(H_e = \frac{n}{n-1}\left(1 - \sum_i p_i^2\right)\) where \(p_i\) is the frequency of haplotype \(i\) and \(n\) is the number of individuals (with non-missing haplotypes).

Shannon entropy (\(H'\))

\(H' = -\sum_i p_i \log_2(p_i)\). Sensitive to both richness and evenness.

Dominant haplotype frequency (\(f_{\max}\))

Frequency of the most common haplotype; high values indicate a selective sweep or strong founder effect.

Usage

compute_haplotype_diversity(haplotypes, missing_string = ".")

Arguments

haplotypes

List as returned by extract_haplotypes.

missing_string

Character. Haplotype strings containing this pattern are treated as missing and excluded from frequency calculations. Default ".".

Value

A data.frame with one row per block and columns: block_id, n_ind (non-missing individuals), n_haplotypes (richness), He (expected heterozygosity), Shannon (entropy in bits), freq_dominant (frequency of most common haplotype).

References

Nei M (1973) Analysis of gene diversity in subdivided populations. PNAS 70(12):3321–3323.

See also

extract_haplotypes, build_haplotype_feature_matrix

Examples

# \donttest{
# (Continuing extract_haplotypes example)
set.seed(1)
geno <- matrix(sample(0:2, 50 * 40, replace = TRUE), 50, 40)
rownames(geno) <- paste0("ind", 1:50)
colnames(geno) <- paste0("rs", 1:40)
snp_info <- data.frame(SNP = colnames(geno), CHR = "chr1",
  POS = seq(1000, by = 5000, length.out = 40))
blocks <- data.frame(start.bp = c(1000, 80000), end.bp = c(70000, 200000), CHR = "chr1")
haps <- extract_haplotypes(geno, snp_info, blocks)
diversity <- compute_haplotype_diversity(haps)
print(diversity)
#>            block_id n_ind n_haplotypes He  Shannon freq_dominant
#> 1  block_1000_70000    50           50  1 5.643856          0.02
#> 2 block_80000_2e+05    50           50  1 5.643856          0.02
# }
